---
title: "What to Watch from AI Engineer World's Fair 2026"
author: "Lawrence Wu"
date: '2026-07-09'
categories: [ai, conference, youtube, agents]
---

AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 just wrapped in San Francisco, and the [AI Engineer YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@aiDotEngineer/videos) has started posting talks. I wanted a quick way to decide what to watch first, so I pulled the current video metadata and ranked the posted talks by views per day. Note this post and all of the code associated with grabbing the data was written by GPT 5.5 (High) using the Codex harness.

The raw view count alone is a little unfair because the uploads are staggered. A talk posted on June 9 has had a month to accumulate views; a talk posted on July 8 has had about a day. So the main ranking below is:

```text
views per day = views / max(1, days since upload)
```

As of July 20, 2026 at 1:48 PM Pacific, the early winners are:

1. [Field Guide to Fable — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9fubhllmsBU)
2. [Understanding is the new bottleneck — Geoffrey Litt, Notion](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WkBPX-oDMnA)
3. [Everything we knew about software has changed — Theo Browne, @t3dotgg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xUnRQ9vLXxo)
4. [The Golden Age of AI Engineering — Alexander Embiricos & Romain Huet & Peter Steinberger, OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pMggiOb18tc)
5. [Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNzCG3lw6O0)

## Method

I included 110 posted conference-talk videos from June 8, 2026 onward. I excluded the 2026 vibe reel, the "6 Things to Know" preview, and scheduled premieres that did not have public view counts yet.

The topic and summary fields are lightweight notes derived from the title, not transcript-level summaries. The popularity ranking is the reproducible part: YouTube metadata, upload date, views, and views per day.

AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 ran June 29 to July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco, according to the [conference schedule](https://www.ai.engineer/schedule).

## Ranked Watchlist

| Rank | Video | Topic | Summary | Views | Views/day |
|---:|---|---|---|---:|---:|
| 1 | [Field Guide to Fable — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9fubhllmsBU) | AI-native product design | A guide to Fable and the product/design patterns behind building with modern AI systems. | 108,272 | 7,733.7 |
| 2 | [Understanding is the new bottleneck — Geoffrey Litt, Notion](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WkBPX-oDMnA) | Code understanding | Argues that understanding what AI-written systems do, not producing code, is the new limiting factor. | 72,327 | 7,232.7 |
| 3 | [Everything we knew about software has changed — Theo Browne, @t3dotgg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xUnRQ9vLXxo) | AI product strategy | Theo Browne frames what is worth building now that AI has changed developer workflows and product expectations. | 79,406 | 6,617.2 |
| 4 | [The Golden Age of AI Engineering — Alexander Embiricos & Romain Huet & Peter Steinberger, OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pMggiOb18tc) | AI engineering keynote | OpenAI leaders discuss why this is a golden age for engineers building with frontier models. | 58,275 | 5,297.7 |
| 5 | [Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UNzCG3lw6O0) | Agent skills | Practical guidance for designing reusable skills that make agents more capable and dependable. | 108,679 | 5,175.2 |
| 6 | [The New Physics of Business — Garry Tan, Y Combinator](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eBUyTS7SzV4) | AI business strategy | Garry Tan argues that AI is rewriting the fundamental economics and dynamics of building a business. | 14,006 | 4,668.7 |
| 7 | [Don't Ship Skills Without Evals — Philipp Schmid, Google DeepMind](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0vphxNt4wyk) | Skill evals | Philipp Schmid argues that agent skills need real evaluation coverage before they ship to production. | 25,988 | 4,331.3 |
| 8 | [From fork() to Fleet: Designing an Agent Sandbox Cloud — Abhishek Bhardwaj, OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OqM67QG_Ikk) | Agent sandboxing | Abhishek Bhardwaj describes designing a cloud sandbox system for safely running many agent processes at scale. | 26,938 | 3,848.3 |
| 9 | [Build Evals That Actually Matter - Nick Ung, Lyft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3z2uT5aDx_Y) | AI evals | Nick Ung shares how to design evaluations that meaningfully measure agent quality instead of vanity metrics. | 3,267 | 3,267.0 |
| 10 | [Stop Burning Tokens: Why self-improvement needs domain expertise first - Annabell Schäfer, Langfuse](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eAXxdtNlK04) | Self-improving agents | Argues agent self-improvement needs domain expertise first before it can stop wasting tokens. | 6,351 | 3,175.5 |
| 11 | ["The engineer of the future is the person who is able to choose what is worth doing." — Addy Osmani](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n97BCfyFIvw) | Engineering judgment | Addy Osmani argues that choosing what is worth building matters more than raw execution speed. | 16,651 | 2,775.2 |
| 12 | [A Practitioner's Guide to Graphs - Tim Ainge, Good Collective](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ySF0I5iE_0) | Graphs for AI | A practitioner's guide to using graph structures in AI applications. | 4,733 | 2,366.5 |
| 13 | [State of the Union: Why Local, Why Now — NVIDIA, Osmantic, Roboflow, EXO Labs, @matthew_berman](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KB41dTlX1Uc) | Local AI | A multi-vendor panel on the case for running AI locally rather than in the cloud. | 17,933 | 1,992.6 |
| 14 | [The Great Loops Debate — Dex Horthy, Geoff Huntley, Ian Livingstone, Greg Pstrucha, @insecure-agents](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c35YoMdnI78) | Agent loop design | A panel debates how the control loops driving AI agents should be structured. | 5,215 | 1,738.3 |
| 15 | [The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=spNAUEgq_A8) | Domain agents | Argues for agents specialized around concrete domains rather than generic assistants. | 35,989 | 1,713.8 |
| 16 | [From Writing Code to Designing Systems: How the Developer Role is Changing — Chris Noring, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GdvKNwMcfd0) | Developer role shift | Discusses how the developer's role is moving from writing code to designing broader systems. | 15,164 | 1,684.9 |
| 17 | [From Blind Spots to Merged PRs: Continuous Agentic Performance Optimization - May Walter, Hud](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JJGbw4ggaFs) | Agentic optimization | May Walter covers continuously optimizing agent performance from blind spots through to merged pull requests. | 1,656 | 1,656.0 |
| 18 | [Forward Deployed Engineering at Cursor — Pauline Brunet](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=APqXGyCoGW4) | Forward-deployed engineering | Pauline Brunet describes how forward-deployed engineers work directly alongside customers at Cursor. | 9,579 | 1,596.5 |
| 19 | [WTF Is the Context Layer? The Missing Infrastructure for Production Agents — Prukalpa Sankar](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8G_1-3IO4ZQ) | Context infrastructure | Prukalpa Sankar defines the context layer as the missing infrastructure production agents actually need. | 8,466 | 1,411.0 |
| 20 | [The UX of AI: Making AI-Powered Apps Your Users Don't Hate - Kathryn Grayson Nanz, Progress Software](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L3RuP_q8Bwc) | AI UX design | Offers UX guidance for building AI-powered apps that users actually enjoy using. | 2,631 | 1,315.5 |
| 21 | [Special Topics in Kernels, RL, Reward Hacking in Agents — Daniel Han, Unsloth](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uIiA6DquRiE) | RL and reward hacking | Daniel Han covers kernels, reinforcement learning, and reward-hacking pitfalls in training agents. | 3,750 | 1,250.0 |
| 22 | ["Software engineering is not about writing code" — Benoit Schillings, Google DeepMind VP of Research](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1P1hJ36rxM0) | Engineering judgment | Benoit Schillings argues software engineering is about problem-solving, not writing code. | 3,079 | 1,026.3 |
| 23 | [Recursive Model Improvement — Lee Robinson, Cursor, SpaceXAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4Tr-DknG2M) | Recursive model improvement | Lee Robinson discusses using models to recursively improve themselves and the tools around them. | 5,031 | 1,006.2 |
| 24 | [Imagination Engineering — Eve Bouffard, Head of Design, Y Combinator](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z2Erdirpudo) | AI product design | Eve Bouffard explores how AI expands what designers can imagine and prototype. | 3,790 | 947.5 |
| 25 | [Modern Post-Training: A Deep Dive  — Will Brown, Prime Intellect](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V-EDrhIhHzQ) | Open AI infrastructure | Will Brown presents the Prime Intellect stack for open, decentralized AI training and infrastructure. | 6,615 | 945.0 |
| 26 | [Claude Fable, Claude Tag, and Anthropic's Culture — Cat Wu & Thariq Shihipar ft Simon Willison](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uU5Gv2h8-9g) | Fireside conversation | Simon Willison talks with Anthropic's Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar about building with AI today. | 4,254 | 850.8 |
| 27 | [We Cut 94% of AI Coding Tokens With a Local Code Index - Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dRmWYHuIJxM) | Code indexing | Shows how local code indexing can sharply reduce token usage in AI coding workflows. | 18,580 | 844.5 |
| 28 | [From Tokens to Cells: Foundation Models for Single-Cell Biology - Akram Baharlouei, Altos Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-561cZmir5Q) | Single-cell biology models | Akram Baharlouei presents foundation models that treat single-cell biology data much like tokens in a language model. | 808 | 808.0 |
| 29 | [Content Is Code - Matt Palmer, Conductor](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yv6xovSsB1U) | Content as code | Frames content itself as a form of code in AI-driven workflows. | 1,569 | 784.5 |
| 30 | [Why Your Agent Disagrees With Itself (And What To Do About It) - Diane Lin, Datadog](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wEc9aG7cRQc) | Agent consistency | Diane Lin examines why an agent can contradict its own earlier answers and how to make its behavior consistent. | 771 | 771.0 |
| 31 | [What if the harness mattered more than the model? - Aditya Bhargava, Etsy](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2e9ANoOEn28) | Evaluation harnesses | Makes the case that the surrounding harness can matter more than model choice for real results. | 9,427 | 725.2 |
| 32 | [Autonomous Agents for Scientific Tasks - Sina Shahandeh, Radicait](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XLEYtv3cMlw) | Scientific agents | Sina Shahandeh describes building autonomous agents that carry out scientific research tasks. | 1,440 | 720.0 |
| 33 | [Computer-Use 2.0: Agents Just Got Multi-Cursor — Francesco Bonacci, Cua](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZSQb5fzRFPw) | Computer-use agents | Francesco Bonacci introduces multi-cursor computer-use agents that can act across several interfaces at once. | 3,510 | 702.0 |
| 34 | [Stop Renting Your Cognitive Infrastructure - Thiyagarajan Maruthavanan, Kalmantic Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Bck7ABCZRZI) | AI ownership | Advocates owning rather than renting the cognitive infrastructure behind AI systems. | 1,399 | 699.5 |
| 35 | [Your Agents Need a Save Button - Hamza Tahir, ZenML](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bZISsg7H7DA) | Agent state persistence | Argues agents need durable checkpointing so their work can be saved and resumed reliably. | 1,301 | 650.5 |
| 36 | [An AI Agent Became the #1 Contributor in OpenAI's Hiring Challenge — Zhengyao Jiang, Weco](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iCj_ATyThvc) | Autonomous coding agents | Zhengyao Jiang recounts how an AI agent topped the leaderboard of OpenAI's hiring challenge. | 2,542 | 635.5 |
| 37 | [You Didn't Ship a Bug. You Just Wrote It for a Human. - Ravi Madabhushi, Scalekit](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lMCxVorb9wM) | Human-facing bugs | Argues that agent mistakes shipped to users are still bugs, just paid for by a human instead of a compiler. | 621 | 621.0 |
| 38 | [HTML is All You Need (for Agents to Make Graphics) - Amol Kapoor, Nori](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JRTAtZ5iBkU) | Agent-generated graphics | Argues that HTML is a strong substrate for agents producing visual outputs. | 13,648 | 620.4 |
| 39 | ["I've never seen anything scarier than an LLM with tool calls." — Erik Meijer aka @HeadinTheBox](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-CnA2lGfymY) | Formal verification for agents | Erik Meijer argues for grounding agent-written code in formal proofs rather than trusting agent behavior directly. | 4,332 | 618.9 |
| 40 | [Agents Need Receipts, Not More Tool Calls - Armanas Povilionis, Alithea Bio](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fu45geO3zX8) | Agent auditability | Argues agents should produce verifiable receipts of their actions rather than simply making more tool calls. | 1,162 | 581.0 |
| 41 | [On AI and Knowledge — Pablo Castro, Distinguished Engineer & CVP for AI Knowledge, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGSFUqzqErE) | AI knowledge systems | Pablo Castro reflects on how AI reshapes the creation and use of organizational knowledge. | 1,719 | 573.0 |
| 42 | [Beyond the Harness: A Journey Towards Adaptative Engineering - Rajiv Chandegra, Annicha Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qdZzND79mcg) | Adaptive engineering | Explores engineering systems that adapt beyond static evaluation harnesses. | 7,244 | 557.2 |
| 43 | [Design Patterns for AI Trust: Juries, Libraries, and Agent Tiers — Alex Bauer, Upside.tech](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YZQsWVeN3rE) | AI trust patterns | Presents design patterns like juries, libraries, and agent tiers for building trustworthy AI systems. | 4,526 | 502.9 |
| 44 | [Agents Need Feature Flags - Sachin Gupta](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zU4EagB311U) | Agent rollout control | Makes the case for feature-flagging agent behavior so rollouts can be controlled safely. | 939 | 469.5 |
| 45 | [Skills are the New SDKs - Elvin Aghammadzada, DataRobot](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LC3-P7v3yoI) | Agent skills | Elvin Aghammadzada argues that reusable skills are becoming the new packaging unit for agent capabilities, replacing SDKs. | 445 | 445.0 |
| 46 | [Enterprise Agents Have a Structure Problem - Ishita Daga, Tesla](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B8l81jhvHbI) | Enterprise agents | Ishita Daga argues enterprise agents fail on structure and organization rather than model quality. | 398 | 398.0 |
| 47 | [Should AI Engineers Still Read Code in 2026? The Z/L Continuum — Alex Volkov, ThursdAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZpK5PWX2YRM) | AI coding practice | Asks how much code AI engineers should still read as agents write more of it. | 3,851 | 385.1 |
| 48 | [Stop AI Agent Hallucinations: 5 Techniques + Production Patterns - Elizabeth Fuentes, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vJukHCIv7Ck) | Agent hallucinations | Presents five production-tested techniques for reducing AI agent hallucinations. | 3,387 | 376.3 |
| 49 | [The Pipeline Is Dead - Iris ten Teije, Sky Valley Ambient Computing](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bRnoEpoK5m4) | Agentic workflows | Reframes traditional software pipelines around more dynamic, ambient AI workflows. | 4,607 | 354.4 |
| 50 | [Don't Let the LLM Drive - Ornella Bahidika & Joel Allou, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m24UKZomm7k) | Agent control flow | Makes the case for keeping deterministic control around the LLM rather than letting the model drive the whole workflow. | 343 | 343.0 |
| 51 | [Can Oncology Workflows Run Without Human Touch? - Anant Shankhdhar, Risa Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_cVfz88_j7A) | Healthcare agents | Anant Shankhdhar asks how far oncology workflows can be automated before a human must stay in the loop. | 332 | 332.0 |
| 52 | [Teaching Coding Agents to do Spreadsheets - Nuno Campos, Witan Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HEFSExa0xl0) | Spreadsheet agents | Covers how to make coding agents useful for spreadsheet-heavy work. | 3,960 | 330.0 |
| 53 | [Building an ACP-Compatible Agent Live — Bennet Fenner, Zed](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HsxQICTLF84) | Agent protocols | A live build showing how an agent can integrate with the Agent Client Protocol ecosystem. | 3,920 | 326.7 |
| 54 | [RLM: Recursive Language Models for Large Codebases - Shashi, Superagentic AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8oyalrfwgjw) | Recursive language models | Introduces recursive language models as a technique for reasoning over very large codebases. | 2,540 | 317.5 |
| 55 | [Every Solo Agent Builder Eventually Reinvents a Worse Version of CI/CD - Sumaiya Shrabony](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WLXxTaPagA8) | Agent tooling | Argues solo builders end up rebuilding CI/CD-like infrastructure for their agents anyway. | 2,849 | 316.6 |
| 56 | [Build AI Systems for Discernment, Not Approval - Angel Ortmann Lee, Duolingo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDqzWpwkSls) | AI product judgment | Advocates building AI systems that help users judge well instead of merely agreeing with them. | 4,048 | 311.4 |
| 57 | [The AI bugpocalypse is here. Now what? - Jack Cable, Corridor](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7JgIS42mz7U) | AI-generated code security | Argues that AI-written code is fueling a surge of security bugs and outlines how to respond. | 2,491 | 311.4 |
| 58 | [Build the AI GTM Agent That Knows the Buyer - Dr. Sajjan Kanukolanu, Position2 (Position Squared)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ltv-L5oMPIs) | Go-to-market agents | Shows how to build a go-to-market agent that understands the buyer well enough to drive real sales motions. | 305 | 305.0 |
| 59 | [Using Spec-Driven Development for Production Workflows - Erik Hanchett, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IddXPepIAS4) | Spec-driven development | Shows how specifications can drive more reliable AI-assisted production workflows. | 6,194 | 281.5 |
| 60 | [Your Voice Agent Doesn't Need a Frontier Model - Joel Allou & Ornella Bahidika, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fnLBmfsI_Fg) | Voice agents | Argues that voice agents can run effectively on smaller models rather than requiring a frontier model. | 274 | 274.0 |
| 61 | [AI System Design: From Idea to Production - Apoorva Joshi, MongoDB](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T0HhO4YtTfE) | AI system design | Walks through moving an AI system from concept to production. | 5,884 | 267.5 |
| 62 | [MCP Apps: Primitives, discovery, and the Future of Software - Pietro Zullo, Manufact, Inc](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sAOBXCDiDOs) | MCP apps | Covers MCP app primitives, discovery, and how software interfaces may evolve. | 3,729 | 248.6 |
| 63 | [Building an Autonomous Engineering Org - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=whue9_YquGA) | Autonomous engineering | Discusses the organizational patterns behind autonomous AI-assisted engineering. | 5,365 | 243.9 |
| 64 | [Your AI Product Will Fail Unless You Can Explain It - Veronica Hylak, Hey AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d_Ftrl3vfV0) | Explainability | Positions explanation as a core product requirement for successful AI systems. | 3,626 | 241.7 |
| 65 | [What Does Done Even Mean? Agents and Paperclip's Liveness Model - Dotta, Paperclip](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7P0elyLIxXo) | Agent task completion | Proposes a liveness model for defining when an agent's task is actually complete. | 1,874 | 234.2 |
| 66 | [The Agentic Web and the Bazaar Era of AI - Ramesh Raskar, MIT Media Lab](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sum9DgexFRQ) | Agentic web | Frames the emerging agentic web as a decentralized "bazaar" era for AI-driven commerce and services. | 1,847 | 230.9 |
| 67 | [The Agentic AI Engineer - Benedikt Sanftl, Mutagent](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pSto5YaNGUo) | Agentic engineering | Defines what changes when AI engineers work through agentic systems. | 4,787 | 228.0 |
| 68 | [Voice Agents That Handle Interrupts - Chintan Agrawal and Daniel Wirjo, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hMlLw1LeIK8) | Voice agents | Covers how to build voice agents that gracefully handle user interruptions in real time. | 227 | 227.0 |
| 69 | [ReviewDebt: a practical framework for scoring every pull request — Sachin Gupta, Ebay](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TJPInBjhE4Q) | Code review process | Introduces a scoring framework for tracking review debt across pull requests. | 1,747 | 218.4 |
| 70 | [Continual Learning for AI Agents: From Failures to Durable Improvements - Soheil Feizi, RELAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2IxD9OB3XuQ) | Continual learning | Looks at how agents can convert failures into durable improvements over time. | 3,198 | 213.2 |
| 71 | [Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fWXJM-J0ZB8) | On-device AI | Covers frontier-quality AI results running closer to the device. | 4,184 | 199.2 |
| 72 | [Agent Output Is Not UX: Rendering Layer Your LLM Pipeline Is Missing - Bala Ramdoss, Amazon Lens](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=maTp79FD9gI) | Agent UX rendering | Argues raw agent output is not UX and that a rendering layer is the missing piece in most LLM pipelines. | 195 | 195.0 |
| 73 | [Your coding agent doesn't always follow your rules — Talha Sheikh, Checkout.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MpZzWMdmQCE) | Agent reliability | Discusses why coding agents drift from instructions and what that means for reliability. | 2,317 | 193.1 |
| 74 | [Semantic Blindness: 500,000 Sensors Confused an LLM - Raahul Singh & Vanč Levstik, Phaidra](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EUsPvBeIx70) | Sensor data / LLM limits | Explores how a massive sensor dataset exposed blind spots in an LLM's semantic understanding. | 1,530 | 191.2 |
| 75 | [When Agents Meet Physical Data: The Other Physics of Agent Harnesses - Dmitry Petrov, DataChain](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bUJgirn4_yc) | Agent data infrastructure | Dmitry Petrov explores what happens when agent harnesses meet large, physical datasets. | 183 | 183.0 |
| 76 | [I Run a Fleet of AI Agents Across Three Machines. Here's What Broke. - Kyle Jaejun Lee, KRAFTON](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4kYl2_mqmnQ) | Multi-agent operations | Lessons from running multiple agents across machines and dealing with the operational failures. | 2,092 | 174.3 |
| 77 | [Chat and citations won't save your vertical AI - Atul Ramachandran, Filed Inc](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGiXcVxSD3s) | Vertical AI product | Argues chat interfaces and citations alone aren't enough to make vertical AI products succeed. | 1,561 | 173.4 |
| 78 | [The Factory That Dreams: 39 AI Agents, No Framework - Rushabh Doshi, Machinecraft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jtzh-GBXBWc) | Multi-agent systems | Describes running 39 AI agents together without relying on an agent framework. | 1,538 | 170.9 |
| 79 | [The Missing Layer After Launch - Raphael Kalandadze, Wandero AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kZsf_Sfm7RU) | Post-launch AI systems | Discusses the operational/product layer needed after an AI product ships. | 2,458 | 163.9 |
| 80 | [Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=APh1Vx0oLmQ) | Agent infrastructure | Discusses infrastructure patterns for making stochastic agents more controllable. | 3,411 | 162.4 |
| 81 | [A Song of Types and Agents - Roberto Stagi, Ratel](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UlFB6efYN5Q) | Type systems for agents | Looks at how strong typing can make agent-built software more reliable. | 1,296 | 162.0 |
| 82 | [Think You Can Build a Game with AI? Think Again! - Danielle An & David Hoe, Meta](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=grdoOC1BT1s) | AI game development | A reality check on the limits and challenges of building games with AI. | 1,892 | 157.7 |
| 83 | [The Prompt Is Still a Punch Card - Ted Johnson, JoinIn AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hVJOnuhFmTA) | Prompting interfaces | Compares prompts to old low-level interfaces and suggests better abstractions are needed. | 2,771 | 153.9 |
| 84 | [remobi.app: Don't change your terminal workflow for mobile](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5192csoTkVo) | Mobile dev tools | Demos remobi.app, which brings your existing terminal workflow to mobile without changing it. | 1,199 | 149.9 |
| 85 | [GTM Is You - Victoria Melnikova, Evil Martians](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G6IlDzj8OjA) | Go-to-market | A builder-oriented view of go-to-market where technical creators carry more of the motion. | 1,926 | 148.2 |
| 86 | [500 people vibe-coded for 30 days. I was one of them. - Sanja Grbic, Automattic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UcYoMg-8-L8) | Vibe coding | Lessons from a month-long large-scale vibe-coding experiment. | 1,905 | 146.5 |
| 87 | [Stop Evaluating Models Like It's the 50s - Alejandro Vidal, Mindmakers](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O3FEoMYvUf8) | Model evaluation | Argues for modernizing how AI models are evaluated beyond outdated benchmarks. | 1,009 | 144.1 |
| 88 | [Develop at Idea Velocity - Jeffrey Lee-Chan, Snapchat](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9arM9b7JgOo) | Development speed | Makes the case for building fast enough to keep pace with ideas rather than process. | 1,141 | 126.8 |
| 89 | [How we taught agents to use good retrieval - Hanna Lichtenberg, Mixedbread AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1IdzkRVmWAA) | Agent retrieval | Explains approaches for making agents use retrieval more effectively. | 1,635 | 125.8 |
| 90 | [Claws Out: Securing and Building with OpenClaw - Nick Taylor, Pomerium](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xg1zNlzw7Jk) | OpenClaw security | Covers security considerations for building on and with the OpenClaw ecosystem. | 1,064 | 118.2 |
| 91 | [Agents Need Receipts, Not More Tool Calls - Armanas Povilionis, Alithea Bio](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q9ycQHbDdJs) | Agent auditability | Argues agents should produce verifiable receipts of their actions rather than simply making more tool calls. | 114 | 114.0 |
| 92 | [Your agent is blindfolded — Johan Lajili, Poolside AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iRcX54EO5g8) | Agent observability | Argues agents need better visibility into their environment to act effectively. | 1,282 | 106.8 |
| 93 | [Structuring the Unstructured - Cedric Clyburn, Red Hat](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-x5GEVnkuRw) | Data structuring | Covers techniques for turning unstructured information into usable structured data. | 2,221 | 101.0 |
| 94 | [SWE-Marathon: Evaluating Coding Agents at Billion-Token Scale - Rishi Desai, Abundant AI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rx8f05JI_WA) | Coding-agent evals | Presents large-scale evaluation of coding agents over very long token horizons. | 1,284 | 98.8 |
| 95 | [Your Agent Failed in Prod. Good Luck Reproducing It. - Tisha Chawla & Susheem Koul, Microsoft](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Lc8zRh9muoY) | Production debugging | Covers the reproducibility challenge when production agents fail. | 1,869 | 89.0 |
| 96 | [Running a Chess YouTube Channel entirely by AI — Stephan Steinfurt, TNG](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BqZrTdgBaPw) | AI media automation | Describes using AI to automate an entire chess YouTube channel workflow. | 1,036 | 86.3 |
| 97 | [Your Agent Is Wasting Tokens and You Don't Know It - Erik Hanchett, AWS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uiP88SpCi1Q) | Token efficiency | Highlights hidden token waste in agent systems and ways to reduce it. | 1,714 | 77.9 |
| 98 | [You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6bmM45jkMDY) | Human skills | Argues for the continued importance of human facilitation and social skill. | 1,624 | 77.3 |
| 99 | [Using RL Agent to Detect and Remediate ETL Pipeline Failures - Anna Marie Benzon](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LrGCT7G_rU8) | RL for data pipelines | Shows reinforcement-learning agents applied to ETL failure detection and remediation. | 1,599 | 76.1 |
| 100 | [Medic for Apache Spark - First Aid for Failing Jobs - Drasko Profirovic, Pinterest](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0RNNfxpdbQk) | Data pipeline debugging | Drasko Profirovic presents a tool that diagnoses and repairs failing Apache Spark jobs. | 76 | 76.0 |
| 101 | [The Prompt is the Platform - Dominik Tornow, Resonate HQ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DqtmZE6Hl0g) | Prompt platforms | Frames prompts as a platform layer for AI applications and workflows. | 1,582 | 75.3 |
| 102 | [Respect The Process - Andrew Dumit, Watershed Technology Inc.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CLttOU7n6sI) | Engineering process | Emphasizes process discipline in AI-enabled engineering workflows. | 908 | 69.8 |
| 103 | [Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=65X0pQ6Lmbg) | Multimodal UX | Covers challenges and promise in voice-to-visual AI workflows. | 1,343 | 61.0 |
| 104 | [Your LLM Deception Monitor Is Broken. The Fix Is in the Training Data - Sachin Kumar, LexisNexis](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IQkVMvXQKLY) | Safety monitoring | Argues that deception monitoring depends heavily on training-data quality. | 668 | 55.7 |
| 105 | [OpenClaw in Your Hand: Building a Physical AI Terminal - Lech Kalinowski, Callstack](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=akk6KRlcwW4) | Physical AI devices | Shows a handheld or physical AI terminal concept built around OpenClaw. | 1,190 | 54.1 |
| 106 | [AI-Driven Multi-Document Correlation for Financial Compliance - Varsha Shah, Independent](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Iwe_RY-fYgI) | Compliance AI | Applies AI to correlating multiple documents for financial compliance workflows. | 1,173 | 53.3 |
| 107 | [User Signal Dies at the Retrieval Boundary - Sonam Pankaj, StarlightSearch](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jx4ZFEAq6bY) | Retrieval signals | Discusses preserving user intent and signals across retrieval system boundaries. | 917 | 41.7 |
| 108 | [When All Context Matters: Extended Cache Augmented Generation - Luis Romero-Sevilla, Orbis](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XovaGv4f39A) | Cache-augmented generation | Covers using extended cache techniques when broad context matters. | 884 | 40.2 |
| 109 | [Bypassing the Multimodal Tax: Hybrid RAG, SQL RRF & UI Telemetry - Abed Matini, Ogilvy](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Akm1sqvWG4A) | Hybrid retrieval | Combines RAG, SQL, reciprocal-rank fusion, and UI telemetry for multimodal workflows. | 830 | 37.7 |
| 110 | [Research to Reality: Bringing Frontier ML Research to Production - Vaidas Razgaitis, Higharc](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OXMMN-XbxwA) | ML productionization | Discusses translating frontier ML research into production systems. | 829 | 37.7 |

## Code to Replicate the Ranking

This script uses [`yt-dlp`](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp) to fetch the channel's current video metadata, filters to recent posted conference talks, and ranks by views per day. Install `yt-dlp` first if needed:

```bash
uv tool install yt-dlp
```

Then run:

```python
import datetime as dt
import json
import subprocess

CHANNEL_URL = "https://www.youtube.com/@aiDotEngineer/videos"
REFERENCE_DATE = dt.date(2026, 7, 20)
PLAYLIST_END = 120

EXCLUDE_TITLE_SUBSTRINGS = [
    "Vibe Reel",
    "Things to Know about AIE",
]

cmd = [
    "yt-dlp",
    "--playlist-end",
    str(PLAYLIST_END),
    "--skip-download",
    "--ignore-errors",
    "--print",
    "%()j",
    CHANNEL_URL,
]

proc = subprocess.run(
    cmd,
    check=False,
    text=True,
    capture_output=True,
    timeout=420,
)

rows = []
for line in proc.stdout.splitlines():
    if not line.startswith("{"):
        continue

    video = json.loads(line)
    title = video.get("title") or ""
    upload_date = video.get("upload_date")
    view_count = video.get("view_count")

    if not upload_date or view_count is None:
        continue
    if upload_date < "20260608":
        continue
    if any(skip in title for skip in EXCLUDE_TITLE_SUBSTRINGS):
        continue

    uploaded = dt.datetime.strptime(upload_date, "%Y%m%d").date()
    days_since_upload = max(1, (REFERENCE_DATE - uploaded).days)
    views_per_day = view_count / days_since_upload

    rows.append(
        {
            "title": title,
            "url": video.get("webpage_url")
            or f"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={video.get('id')}",
            "upload_date": uploaded.isoformat(),
            "views": view_count,
            "days_since_upload": days_since_upload,
            "views_per_day": views_per_day,
            "duration_minutes": round((video.get("duration") or 0) / 60, 1),
        }
    )

rows.sort(key=lambda row: row["views_per_day"], reverse=True)

print("| Rank | Video | Uploaded | Views | Days | Views/day |")
print("|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|")

for rank, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
    print(
        "| {rank} | [{title}]({url}) | {upload_date} | "
        "{views:,} | {days_since_upload} | {views_per_day:,.1f} |".format(
            rank=rank,
            **row,
        )
    )
```

A couple of notes:

- `--ignore-errors` lets the script skip scheduled premieres that do not have watchable metadata yet.
- `PLAYLIST_END = 120` was enough for this snapshot because the recent conference batch appeared in the first 120 channel videos.
- The views are a snapshot. Re-running the script later will produce different view counts and a different views/day ranking.
